authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.15 - Condition of participation: Emergency preparedness
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital emergency-preparedness regulation. It directly supports downtime, continuity, communication, training, and escalation expectations for operational disruption handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.23 - Condition of participation: Nursing services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital nursing-services regulation. It supports escalation when staffing adequacy, licensed nurse availability, or patient-care assignment authority is implicated.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation. It anchors the medical record and auditability baseline when patient, encounter, or operational records are used.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Its guidance constrains PHI use and disclosure for treatment, payment, and health care operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling and privacy-sensitive operations routing

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance determines when operational outputs involving PHI require limited disclosure, role-based handling, and privacy escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization guidance is authoritative for payer/provider authorization workflow expectations and supports escalation when scheduled work is blocked by authorization status.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization, referral, and eligibility prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Incident Command System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public state healthcare emergency-services authority guidance for hospital incident command. It grounds operational escalation roles, situation status, resource tracking, and surge or downtime command posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capacity, resource, incident, and command escalation

authoritative_sources.title: TeamSTEPPS Two-Day Training: Communication Tools
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and patient-safety agency. TeamSTEPPS huddle guidance grounds brief operational status exchange, plan adjustment, and escalation language.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: daily huddle and operational status exchange

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Inpatient Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS hospital quality reporting is an authoritative public program for patient-flow and quality-measure context, including ED throughput concepts used to frame operational delay and capacity scenarios.

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, care team, procedure, and clinical note terminology used by EHR dashboards and operational systems.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant/facility/service-line context, source-of-truth hierarchy, current huddle/capacity/staffing/schedule/system evidence, RACI, escalation SLA, downtime procedure, privacy posture, and, when patient- or scheduled-work-specific, patient/member status, service date, referral, eligibility, authorization, and documentation state. Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility blocks operational completion and must route to patient access or utilization management.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include the EHR or medical record, ADT feed, EHR operational dashboards, bed/capacity management tool, scheduling system, staffing or workforce system, referral/authorization/eligibility record, payer or portal evidence where tenant-approved, ITSM ticket, document repository, privacy/audit system, and tenant source hierarchy. Claim or payer truth remains with revenue cycle, utilization management, patient access, or payer operations rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, triage, clinical protocol change, treatment planning, discharge readiness, patient-specific priority, licensure or scope-of-practice staffing decisions, PHI disclosure approval, medical necessity, coverage, authorization approval, appeal, coding, claim disposition, legal interpretation, or executive resource approval. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Operational intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture facility, service line, unit or clinic, operating date, requested operational action, source systems, RACI, escalation SLA, and authority context before accepting work.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate daily huddle notes, throughput tracker, bed or capacity board, staffing roster, schedule, ITSM ticket, downtime procedure, authorization/referral/eligibility status, privacy constraints, and source-of-truth hierarchy.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle execution and status progression
workflow_stages.description: Move accepted work through prerequisites_checked, in_progress, blocked, escalated, completed, or returned_to_orchestrator based on auditable evidence and owner acceptance.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Prioritize capacity, staffing, system, access, scheduled-work, privacy, clinical-boundary, payer, policy, or documentation exceptions by service impact, downstream delay, patient-safety signal, and accountable owner.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Record completion only when source record, owner acceptance, residual risks, reopen trigger, downstream handoff, and audit trail are documented.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: daily throughput or capacity status update
authorization, referral, or eligibility prerequisite note
systems_of_record: eligibility system or approved payer portal evidence
privacy, consent, access-control, and audit systems

allowed: Prioritize operational blockers based on documented throughput, access, staffing, capacity, system, scheduled-work, and downstream-delay impact.
Determine when a capacity, staffing, system, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, or source-record blocker requires escalation to the accountable owner.
Prepare operational exception summaries, daily status updates, downtime notes, and completion evidence from available records.
Maintain reversible lifecycle states for accepted operational work when evidence supports the transition.
escalate: Escalate clinical protocol, patient-specific priority, treatment direction, discharge readiness, or patient-safety interpretation to clinical leadership or licensed review.
Escalate staffing licensure, skill mix, credentialing, or patient assignment safety to staffing office, nursing leadership, or appropriate licensed owner.
Escalate PHI disclosure, broad patient-level exports, consent/proxy ambiguity, or minimum-necessary uncertainty to healthcare privacy or compliance.

Escalate coverage, authorization approval, medical necessity, denial, coding, claim, reimbursement, or payer policy interpretation to patient access, utilization management, revenue cycle, or payer operations.
Escalate public-source, tenant-policy, payer-rule, facility-procedure, or jurisdictional conflicts to orchestrator and accountable governance owner.
refuse: Refuse to diagnose, triage, change clinical protocols, provide treatment direction, or set patient-specific clinical priority.
Refuse to decide staffing licensure, scope-of-practice coverage, credentialing, or final staff assignment safety.
Refuse to disclose PHI or export patient-level operational data outside tenant role-based access, consent, minimum-necessary, and audit controls.
Refuse to approve coverage, authorization, claim disposition, reimbursement approach, coding, appeal, or payer policy interpretation.
Refuse to mark work complete without system-of-record evidence, owner acceptance, residual-risk handling, and closure criteria.

domain_failure_modes: Patient or member harm from bad routing, missed escalation, unsupported clinical-priority assumptions, or delayed capacity response.
PHI exposure from broad bed-board, schedule, or capacity exports without minimum-necessary controls.
Revenue leakage, claim denial, or compliance exposure from proceeding despite missing referral, authorization, eligibility, or documentation prerequisites.
Unsafe staffing or licensure assumptions when operations treats a roster gap as authority to decide scope or patient assignments.
Downtime or system outage handled without manual evidence, ITSM trail, privacy safeguards, or continuity procedure.
Incorrect completion record when source systems conflict or owner acceptance is missing.
Generic operations behavior that ignores healthcare-specific EHR, ADT, capacity, staffing, PHI, clinical, and payer boundaries.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Capacity constraints create downstream delays across multiple teams
An operational issue appears clinical but only parts are within administrative scope
A system or staffing blocker prevents completion of scheduled work
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources cannot identify a tenant facility's authoritative EHR dashboard, capacity tool, staffing system, escalation SLA, or RACI. The pack requires retrieval or tenant configuration at runtime and refuses final disposition when these facts are missing.

State licensure, facility policy, union contracts, and medical staff bylaws may impose stricter requirements. The lane records the gap and escalates rather than deciding licensure or patient assignment authority.

Public CMS guidance supplies baseline workflow concepts, but payer, plan, product, provider type, facility type, and service setting determine operational prerequisites. The lane routes interpretation to patient access, utilization management, revenue cycle, or payer operations.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because tenant-specific variables are explicitly required runtime inputs and refusal conditions.

Research output constrained the final pack by requiring healthcare-specific systems of record, PHI controls, authorization/referral/eligibility prerequisite handling, clinical-versus-administrative boundaries, downtime procedures, and completion evidence.
